tasks.title: Project scaffold + CueKeeperEntitlements package + App Group + negative-import gate
acceptance_criteria: Single app target + CueKeeperEntitlements local package build clean
App Group container id defined and attached to the app target's entitlements
A throwaway 'import CueKeeperEntitlements' added inside a stub UI/LiveRun/ file causes a build failure, then is removed — proof captured in commit message

tasks.title: Core: Event + Cue
acceptance_criteria: Event and Cue are Sendable, Codable, compile under Swift 6 strict concurrency
Cue.duration == 0 is valid ('instant' cue)
backupTargetCueID stored as UUID, never index
Round-trip Codable test passes via Swift Testing

tasks.title: Core: ActionLogEntry + EventLog
acceptance_criteria: ActionLogEntry cases carry both wall-clock Date and monotonic uptime
EventLog is append-only in design intent (no truncation API exposed)
Codable round-trip test passes

tasks.title: Core: ClockProviding + SystemClock
acceptance_criteria: ClockProviding protocol has now()/uptime() with no default implementation leaking real time
SystemClock is the only production conformance; tests use a fake conformance

tasks.title: Core: LiveRunProjection + LiveRunEngine
acceptance_criteria: LiveRunEngine.project is a pure static function: (Event, EventLog, ClockProviding) -> LiveRunProjection, no side effects

Swift Testing @Test(arguments:) suite is green for: hold-rebase + drift reset, undo-after-skip, undo-after-jump-to-backup, jump-to-backup pointer relocation (skipped cues stay visible), zero-duration cue handling, clock-jump threshold detection, last-cue terminal states for all four controls, hold-on-last-cue-unresolved staying on-hold
LiveRunProjection is Equatable

tasks.title: Core: EventStatus + EventManifest
acceptance_criteria: EventStatus is derived, never stored as an independent flag anywhere in Core
EventManifest.activeLiveEventID is documented as a hint requiring re-projection before trust

tasks.title: Persistence: EventStore actor, manifest, atomic writes, corruption isolation
acceptance_criteria: All writes are temp-file-then-atomic-rename
resolveActiveLiveEvent() re-projects the log via LiveRunEngine.project before trusting the manifest pointer
Per-event corruption quarantines one row; manifest corruption is a distinct, worse app-root error path
Round-trip and corruption tests green
A test reads back the actual file-protection attribute on disk and asserts .completeUntilFirstUserAuthentication — not assumed from config

tasks.title: ClockJumpDetector
acceptance_criteria: Pure function taking previous/current wall+uptime and a tolerance, returns Bool
Table-driven Swift Testing cases cover both a real clock jump and normal small drift not falsely flagging

tasks.title: NotificationScheduler with windowed scheduling
acceptance_criteria: Scheduled window capped at next 20 cues or 2-hour lookahead, whichever is smaller
rescheduleAll cancels and reschedules only that window, not the full remaining tail, on every mutating action
Requests keyed by eventID+cueID
.timeSensitive interruption level set on every request
Test asserts pending-request count stays well under the 64 OS ceiling for a 40-cue event

tasks.title: HapticsService + HapticPattern
acceptance_criteria: HapticsService explicitly marked @MainActor
Four distinguishable patterns: advance, hold, skip, contingencyReveal
Documented call-contract: await any persistence write first, then hop to main to fire the haptic — never called from off-actor code

tasks.title: EntitlementService + EntitlementResult (CueKeeperEntitlements package)
acceptance_criteria: checkEntitlement() races Transaction.currentEntitlements against a 2s Task.sleep timeout
Timeout and any unverifiable state map to .unverifiable, treated identically to .free everywhere downstream
Cache refreshed via Transaction.updates at launch/foreground
Local .storekit config drives a test proving fail-open behavior on timeout

tasks.title: AccessibilityAnnouncer utility
acceptance_criteria: Thin wrapper around UIAccessibility.post(.announcement:), no dependency on any console-specific type
Standalone new file only — does not edit any file owned by another lane
Available early enough that T-016's undo-banner/clock-warning announcements can call it directly rather than inlining the same logic

tasks.title: AppPhase + AppCoordinator root routing
acceptance_criteria: Root is a phase switch (.loadingPersistence/.console/.home), never a NavigationStack redirect
bootstrap() resolves the active live event via EventStore before setting phase — never renders Home first and redirects
Force-quit mid-cue + relaunch lands directly in the console with correct projected state
Attempting a second live run surfaces the resume-or-end conflict rather than silently starting one

tasks.title: Creation Flow: Home
acceptance_criteria: True-first-launch-empty, later-zero-events, populated-list, live-event-present, and entitlement-blocked-at-creation states all render distinctly
Live-event-present state demotes other rows and routes taps to the conflict dialog instead of Builder
This is the only primary_ui file group allowed to import CueKeeperEntitlements

tasks.title: Creation Flow: Builder + Cue Edit (including its own accessibility)
acceptance_criteria: Full CRUD: add/edit/delete/duplicate/drag-reorder, seeded-sample and blank-empty states both render via the same empty-state prompt component
Deleting a cue that is another cue's backup target triggers an explicit confirmation dialog, never a silent orphan or a permanent block
Negative duration blocked inline as the stepper moves
Independently-timed (not self-timed) build-to-ready pass measured under two minutes
Drag-reorder ships explicit VoiceOver 'Move up'/'Move down' custom actions alongside onMove, written as part of this same task — not a separate later pass on this file
Layout holds at maximum Dynamic Type without clipping or truncating

tasks.title: Live Console + Completion (including its own accessibility)
acceptance_criteria: View bodies read only LiveRunProjection, never the raw Event/EventLog
TickingNumeral takes only the two Dates it needs so its per-second tick never invalidates the control bar's identity
No file in this task's list imports CueKeeperEntitlements — build error if attempted, verified structurally not just by review
Contingency reveal expands in-place from the control bar, never a sheet; auto-collapses on any advance/hold/skip tap
Completion screen shows a real computed final drift number and completed-vs-skipped summary
VoiceOver reading order is deliberate: current cue -> elapsed/remaining -> drift/hold -> next cue -> controls, written as part of this same task
Every control has a consequence-specific label ('Skip to next cue', never 'Button')
Undo banner does not auto-dismiss on a fixed timer when isVoiceOverRunning is true and announces its appearance via AccessibilityAnnouncer
Console contrast verified in its fixed-dark state, especially warning/hold, not eyeballed

tasks.title: Interruption surfaces: live-run conflict, notification priming, paywall sheet
acceptance_criteria: Notification permission requested contextually at first Start Live tap, never during onboarding; denial never blocks the workflow
Paywall sheet shows success / fail-open-to-free / brief inline loading (<2s) states, fail-open never looks like an error
Entitlement gating fires only at the two creation-time boundaries, never mid-run

tasks.title: seedSampleEventForUITesting + XCUITest tap-count suite
acceptance_criteria: -uiTestSeedSampleEvent launch argument drops the app directly into a known live-console state
XCUITest actually counts taps and asserts advance/hold/skip/contingency-jump are each <=2 taps from the default running state
Cold-launch-into-active-run and one-live-run-conflict have explicit UI test coverage

tasks.title: SnapshotPrivacyGuard + TemplateExporter (should-have)
acceptance_criteria: SnapshotPrivacyGuard is attempted only after core hardening (T-016/T-018) is solid; if cut for time, FINAL_REPORT.md must state this as an explicit yes/no line, not an open question
TemplateExporter strips contingencyNote and role free text by default, includeNotes is an explicit opt-in
This task adds new, standalone files only — it does not edit any file already owned by primary_ui

tasks.title: Physical-device verification checklist + final report assembly
acceptance_criteria: Checklist executed on a real device: lock/unlock mid-cue, force-quit+relaunch, full airplane-mode pass across build/run/recover, deliberate clock-change warning, denied-notification run, four haptic patterns confirmed distinct, VoiceOver reorder + undo persistence, max Dynamic Type
Final report states the enumerated, measured tap counts for advance/hold/skip/contingency-jump, answering the invalidation criterion directly
Any should-have item cut under time pressure (Live Activities, Widgets, App Intents, snapshot blur, template export) is named explicitly, never silently dropped
